COSMOLOGY / INTRO

THE OBSERVABLE UNIVERSE

13.8 billion years old, expanding across 93 billion light-years, holding trillions of galaxies.

YOU ARE HERE
COSMOLOGY / MAP

OUR SOLAR SYSTEM

Concentric gravity bands pulling eight planets and thousands of small bodies in endless orbits around our host star.

Distance: 287.46 AU
•
Age: 4.6B Years
•
1 Star • 8 Planets
THE HOST STAR

THE SUN

A massive thermodynamic engine of hydrogen plasma fusing into helium, yielding 99.86% of the solar system's entire mass.

Core Temp: 15,000,000°C
•
Surface Temp: 5,500°C
PLANET / MERCURY

MERCURY

The swift messenger, closest to the Sun, experiencing extreme temperature swings from boiling days to freezing nights.

Diameter4,879 km
Day Length58.6 days
Distance0.39 AU
Surface °C430° / -180°
PLANET / VENUS

VENUS

A hellish greenhouse world shrouded in toxic sulfuric clouds, hot enough to melt lead on its volcano-scarred surface.

Diameter12,104 km
Day Length243 days
Distance0.72 AU
Surface °C465°C
PLANET / EARTH

EARTH

A vibrant oasis of liquid water, oxygen-rich skies, and diverse biospheres — the only world known to harbor life.

Diameter12,742 km
Day Length24 hours
Distance1.00 AU
Life8.7M Species
PLANET / MARS

MARS

The rusty, desert planet of towering volcanoes and dry riverbeds, standing as humanity's primary frontier for colonization.

Diameter6,779 km
Day Length24h 37m
Distance1.52 AU
Olympus Mons21.9 km
ORBIT DEBRIS

ASTEROID BELT

A sweeping circular ring of ancient rocky rubble drifting between Mars and Jupiter. Over 1 million rocks larger than 1km.

Distance: 1.1 to 3.3 AU
•
Collision frequency: ~8.3s
PLANET / JUPITER

JUPITER

A massive gas giant of swirling storm bands and atmospheric vortices, dominated by the ancient Great Red Spot.

Diameter139,820 km
Day Length9h 56m
Distance5.20 AU
Moons95
PLANET / SATURN

SATURN

The crown jewel of the solar system, famous for its majestic, icy ring system and gaseous golden clouds.

RING SYSTEM EXPLORER
Viewing the full Saturnian system. Spanning 282,000 km, the rings are 99% pure water ice particles, ranging from micro-dust to massive blocks.
Diameter116,460 km
Ring Span282,000 km
Distance9.58 AU
Moons146
PLANET / URANUS

URANUS

An ice giant rotating on its side, encircled by vertical rings and swept by extreme planetary winds.

Diameter50,724 km
Axial Tilt97.77°
Distance19.19 AU
Temp-224°C
PLANET / NEPTUNE

NEPTUNE

A dark, frozen wind-swept world of methane storms, orbiting at the edge of our solar system's planetary zone.

Diameter49,244 km
Wind Speed2,100 km/h
Distance30.07 AU
Orbit165 years
THE DEEP REGIONS

KUIPER BELT

A vast freezing twilight zone of ice crystals and frozen dwarf planets floating beyond Neptune.

Pluto
Size: 2,376 km
Famous heart-marked ice plains of Tombaugh Regio.
Eris
Size: 2,326 km
Massive outer dwarf planet covered in methane ice.
Haumea
Size: ~1,600 km
Elongated dwarf planet spinning rapidly every 4 hours.
BEYOND THE SUN

INTERSTELLAR SPACE

The heliopause — where the solar wind ends and the cosmic ray sea begins. Voyager 1 wanders alone.

23,000,000,000 km
•
Heliopause crossed: 2012
COSMOLOGY / OUTSIDE

NEARBY STARS

Hover points to scan our stellar neighbors. Slow camera drift showing 3D coordinates relative to Earth.

GALACTIC HOME

THE MILKY WAY

A vast rotating spiral arms galaxy spanning 100,000 light-years. Our Orion Arm revolves 26,000 ly from the core.

SINGULARITY

BLACK HOLES

Sagittarius A* — 4 million solar masses. Intense gravity bending light into an accretion disk with doppler shift.

SINGULARITY METRICS
Polar Jet Ejection
Viewing Sagittarius A*. A supermassive black hole. The strong gravitational force bends space-time, pulling light from the accretion disk into a double-lensed halo.
COSMIC CHRONOLOGY

COSMIC TIMELINE

A 13.8 billion years path from singularity to present era. Drag scrubber to travel through cosmic epochs.

Drag the handle along the timeline to observe the milestones of cosmic expansion.
Big Bang
First Light
First Stars
Solar System
Life
Now
SIZE PERSPECTIVE

SCALE OF THINGS

Observe relative dimensions: from humans to Everest, Earth, the Sun, Betelgeuse, and the Universe itself.

Human (1.7m)
Mt Everest (8.8km)
Earth (12,742km)
Sun (1.39M km)
Betelgeuse (1.2B km)
Observable Universe

Every star you see is a sun.

Many have planets.

Some may have life.

You are made of stardust.

spaceedu
